name: Shiroka Laka
description: A village famous for its bagpipe school and traditional Rhodope architecture. Stone houses line cobbled streets unchanged for 200 years.

name: Kovachevitsa
description: One of Bulgaria's most preserved medieval villages. Fortress-like stone houses, no cars allowed inside. Time stands still here.

name: Leshten
description: A tiny Rhodope hamlet with panoramic views over the valley. Restored stone guesthouses, wild herb gathering, and total silence.

name: Bozhentsi
description: An architectural reserve in the Balkan foothills. Icon painting, rose oil production, national revival-era stone houses.

name: Zheravna
description: Bulgaria's largest architectural reserve. Chestnut forests, water mills, authentic taverns. Inspiration for Yordan Yovkov's literature.

name: Koprivshtitsa
description: The town that sparked the April Uprising of 1876. A living museum of Bulgarian history with six house-museums and colorful facades.

name: Melnik
description: Bulgaria's smallest town. Famous for Melnik wine and unique sand pyramids. Thracian, Greek, and Bulgarian layers of history.

name: Tryavna
description: The capital of Bulgarian wood carving. The Tryavna school produced master craftsmen whose work fills monasteries across Bulgaria.

name: Stoilovo
description: Deep in the ancient Strandzha forest. The last village practicing Nestinarstvo — fire-walking on live coals, a UNESCO-recognized tradition.

name: Teteven
description: Gateway to Teteven Balkan. Rock formations, waterfalls, and a bear sanctuary nearby. Hayduk history and traditional yogurt culture.
